seo.title: Building Customer Support with Vercel AI SDK + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build customer support with Vercel AI SDK + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building customer support agents with Vercel AI SDK enables real-time, streaming conversational UIs powered by leading LLMs, perfect for handling inquiries, ticket creation, and order lookups. However, in high-risk scenarios like RAG agents with CRM integration, failures such as partial hallucinations, unvalidated tool calls, and inconsistent outputs can lead to misinformation, policy violations, or PII leaks. Vex provides essential guardrails to verify response accuracy, filter sensitive data, ensure tone consistency, and trigger escalations before responses reach customers.

architectureOverview.heading: Customer Support Architecture with Vercel AI SDK
architectureOverview.description:
The typical architecture starts with a React Server Component or Next.js API route using Vercel AI SDK's `generateText` or `streamText` to power a conversational UI. User queries trigger a RAG pipeline: semantic search against a knowledge base (e.g., Vercel Postgres or Pinecone), followed by LLM augmentation with retrieved context. The LLM may invoke tools for CRM actions like ticket creation via HubSpot/Zendesk APIs or order lookup in Stripe/Shopify, with responses streamed back to the client for low-latency UX.

Tool calling introduces risks as results must be validated before display, and edge functions can timeout on complex multi-step reasoning. Structured output parsing varies across providers (OpenAI vs Anthropic), requiring normalization. PII from CRM data demands filtering, while tone and policy compliance ensure brand safety.

risks.name: Streaming responses with partial hallucinations
risks.description: Incomplete RAG context causes fabricated details about orders or policies mid-stream
risks.mitigation: Vex verifies factual accuracy against knowledge base before streaming chunks

risks.name: Tool call results not validated before display
risks.description: Unverified CRM data (e.g., wrong order details) shown to customers
risks.mitigation: Vex schema validates tool outputs and cross-checks against source APIs

risks.name: Inconsistent structured output across providers
risks.description: OpenAI vs Anthropic tool call formats break ticket creation parsing
risks.mitigation: Vex normalizes structured outputs to consistent TypeScript interfaces

risks.name: Edge function timeouts on complex agent tasks
risks.description: Multi-tool CRM workflows exceed Vercel edge limits, dropping conversations
risks.mitigation: Vex async verification offloads heavy checks, preserving streaming perf
